comment_body:
Actively using a shell-based orchestration setup I built called BUILD_SCRIPT (https://github.com/stefanocasafranca/build-script). The core idea: instead of babysitting Claude through a UI, you define your agent tasks in structured scripts and let the whole thing run with minimal interruption.

What I found works well in practice:
- Break the work into discrete, verifiable steps. Claude handles each step, the script validates output before moving on.
- Keep context lean per step. Stuffing everything into one giant prompt kills quality.
- Log every action. When something breaks, you know exactly where.

The workflow that clicked for me is coding tasks where you have a clear spec. Feed the spec, let the agents work through it in sequence, review the diff at the end.

Would genuinely love to hear what you built for multi-agent coding. Are you doing parallel agents or sequential? That is the design decision I keep revisiting. Parallel is faster but harder to keep consistent state across agents.
